vitals.terrain: alpine roads with six passes above 2000m

course_description.overview: Epic alpine gran fondo with long route (166.8 km, 4300 m gain) crossing six high passes including Umbrail, Foscagno, Eira, Forcola, Bernina, and Fuorn; short route (106.5-106.8 km, 2050 m gain) covers Forcola, Bernina, and Eira. Starts in Aquagranda, finishes at Mottolino Gondola top station.

key_segments.name: Passo dell’Umbrail
key_segments.description: Legendary climb above 2500m on long route, unique to this gran fondo.

key_segments.name: Passo del Foscagno
key_segments.description: High alpine pass on long route.

key_segments.name: Passo Eira
key_segments.description: Final climb to Mottolino finish, featured in Giro d'Italia, with special 'Pogacar Segment' prize.

key_segments.name: Passo della Forcola
key_segments.description: Early climb on both routes leading to Swiss Bernina scenery.

key_segments.name: Passo del Bernina
key_segments.description: Breathtaking Swiss pass on both routes.

key_segments.name: Passo dal Fuorn
key_segments.description: Technical pass on long route through Swiss Engadin.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: One of Europe's toughest gran fondos with relentless high-altitude climbing above 2000m, lowest point 1300m; demands strong endurance and altitude acclimatization.

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Milan/Malpensa or Bergamo, then bus/train to Livigno; event in duty-free zone near Swiss/Engadin border.
logistics.accommodation: Abundant in Livigno, Europe's cycling capital with training camps; book early due to 2026 Olympics proximity.
logistics.registration_details: Open Nov 3, 2025 via ENDU portal; early-bird €50 until Jan 6, 2026, increasing fees until June 24; in-person June 26. FCI members 17+ only.
logistics.bike_logistics: Start Aquagranda Via Rasia 999, 7am departure; finish Mottolino with food party (pizzoccherata).
logistics.local_considerations: High altitude (Livigno ~1800m+), weather variable; duty-free shopping; ties into 2026 Olympic host town vibe.

final_verdict.one_liner: Ultimate high-alpine road suffering with notable scenery and climbs.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong road cyclists seeking epic multi-pass challenges; altitude-adapted endurance riders.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for the prestige, views, and rarity of Umbrail in a gran fondo.
pro_tips: Acclimatize in Livigno a week prior for altitude.
Train long climbs; Eira finish is brutal.
Pack layers for Swiss/Italian alpine weather shifts.
